title: Avsnitt 584 - Greed is not good
summary_md:
# Avsnitt 584 - Greed is not good

**Feed**: fill-or-kill  
**Published**: 2026-06-09  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode 584 of Hill or Kill focuses on the SpaceX IPO frenzy sucking liquidity from markets, the speculative hype in Swedish small-caps (Sivers, Smoltek) and biotech (AlzeCure's Eli Lilly deal), and the psychology of greed when positions go your way. The hosts discuss Eutelsat as a cheap space/defense infrastructure option, remain long iGaming names (Evolution, Kambi) into the football World Cup, and close with a CEO interview on payments firm Kliro. Crypto stays cold while AI/IPO names dominate.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 ETL — Eutelsat | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Host holds large position; market prices it as a dying Viasat while its LEO business grows ~60%; #2 LEO operator after Starlink.

#### Reasoning

Asymmetric option-like play: downside seen as limited because the stock is valued as a melting ice cube, while the fast-growing LEO segment is not yet visible in numbers. Host expects a re-rating as critical European space/defense infrastructure around the SpaceX IPO, given EU's need for sovereign launch and LEO capability and rejection of US dependence.

#### Sell Triggers

- if LEO growth fails to show in financials at scale
- if SpaceX IPO sell-flow keeps draining space names without re-rating
- if Europe walks over the satellite sector to the US

> *"de värderar ju bolaget som att de är ett viasatt som håller på en smältande isbit. Därför tycker jag hela det här spacetemat... det är en väldigt billig option"*

---

### 🟢 EVO — Evolution | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Both hosts long as a summer position; ~100M SEK/day buybacks ('unheard of') with a weak stock expected to grind higher; World Cup tailwind.

#### Reasoning

Massive daily buybacks (~170k shares / ~100M SEK) are absorbing heavy sell flow; hosts believe sellers must eventually be exhausted, setting up a steady grind higher over the summer. Football World Cup deposits feed into casino crossover play. Conviction tempered by surprise at persistent supply.

#### Sell Triggers

- if buyback is reduced or paused
- if sell flow continues without exhaustion
- if World Cup engagement fails to convert to casino play

> *"Jag har köpt på mig Eva här som en sommarposition. Och det borde bara tugga 5 kronor om dagen här"*

---

### 🟢 KAMBI — Kambi | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 60%

Host long into football World Cup with many matches; buybacks paused but may restart after June AGM; new-customer acquisition tailwind.

#### Reasoning

World Cup brings a heavy match schedule and a wave of new customers that tend to be retained and reactivated into later events (tennis, Wimbledon, Premier League). Paused buyback has let the stock drift lower; June AGM could enable a restart, adding support. Sector focus on gambling names is expected to pick up.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AGM fails to re-enable buyback
- if World Cup customer acquisition disappoints
- if gambling-sector weakness persists

> *"Och som avslutning då, fotbolls-VM. Jag har ju Kambi. Det känns som att det blir större och större."*

---

### 🔴 SIVE — Sivers Semiconductors | **SELL** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Host is short from 94 expecting a crash; instead stock held up despite Friday's chip selloff, then a fresh $8M space order pushed it down ~5%.

#### Reasoning

Clear hype name (up to 100+) where logic has broken down: a placing at 14.50 diluted holders, an insider sold ~100M SEK before a $8M order, and index inclusion (MSCI/SPX) drives flows. Host shorted expecting a crash but admits the risk/reward of being short is poor when nothing behaves logically; conviction is low and speculative.

#### Sell Triggers

- if continued index/ETF inflows squeeze the short
- if more real orders sustain the narrative
- if broad chip/momentum strength resumes

> *"då börjar man ju frågasätta, vad är riskrevården på att vara kort sådana här grejer när ingenting stämmer längre?"*

---

### 🟠 SMOL — Smoltek | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 40%

Rights issue while stock up 1000%; cited as evidence of pure speculative hype in micro-caps with no institutional support, only retail.

#### Reasoning

Illustrates low risk-sentiment small-cap dynamics: with no institutions, retail sets exchange prices far above what guarantors/garanter would pay, so an offensive raise should ideally be a rights issue to avoid diluting bullish holders. Hosts explicitly label the area a hype, signaling caution rather than conviction either way.

#### Sell Triggers

- if hype unwinds and retail support fades
- if dilution terms prove unfavorable
- if no fundamental orders materialize

> *"kursen har gått 1000%. Och det visar väl hur det funkar i de här mindre bolagen så finns det inga institutioner"*

---

### ⛔ OBDU-B — Obducat | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 50%

Directed emission at an extreme discount (~0.50 vs ~1.00) brought in holders who immediately dumped tens of millions of shares ~40% down.

#### Reasoning

Hosts criticize the directed placement as favoring certain owners at a huge discount, attracting non-aligned participants who flipped shares for a quick ~0.10 SEK/share gain rather than building positions. The resulting supply overhang and signal about shareholder quality make it an avoid in the near term.

#### Sell Triggers

- if overhang from emission buyers clears
- if a more disciplined shareholder base emerges

> *"någon beredd att sälja 40% ner. Så aktien handlades i en krona. Och sen på en sekund så ställde någon upp tiotals miljoner aktier. På 60 öre."*

---

### 🟡 ALZCUR — AlzeCure (All Secure) | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 40%

Signed Eli Lilly deal ($10M upfront, up to ~$1bn milestones) sending stock up ~300%; congratulatory tone, value now hinges on the option as the asset advances.

#### Reasoning

Hosts see the preclinical Eli Lilly deal as validation and great for Swedish biotech risk sentiment, but the $10M is earmarked for other projects so the case becomes how the market values the milestone option. Value should rise as the asset enters and clears clinical phases; pipeline is thin since much has failed. Positive but already-moved, hence hold/watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the Eli Lilly asset fails to advance into clinical phases
- if remaining pipeline disappoints
- if dilution from the ongoing rights issue pressures the stock

> *"de signade ett avtal med Eli Lilly. 10 miljoner dollar up front och sen en miljard i milestones. Och det är ju en fantastisk nyhet."*

---

### 🟠 CLIRO — Kliro | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Payments/BNPL firm just reached profitability, growing ~39% (38% Q1), 5-10% higher checkout conversion; oversubscribed emission yet stock stalls on an outdated market view.

#### Reasoning

CEO interview frames a turnaround back to the core payments business with strong conversion performance and improving consumer experience (30%+ fewer support cases two years running). Hosts note the misconception is a stale low-growth image plus a lag before volume converts to revenue given the payment-method-dependent model. Constructive but unproven in the share price, hence watch.

#### Sell Triggers

- if revenue fails to follow processed-volume growth
- if growth momentum below ~38% decelerates
- if payment-method mix keeps margins low

> *"Nu växer vi ungefär 39% förra året och 38% i Q1... Samtidigt har det tagit lite tid att det ska generera intäkter."*

---
